questions.prompt:
A student studies how pH affects the activity of catalase, an enzyme found in many living cells. Catalase breaks down hydrogen peroxide into water and oxygen. Four test tubes contain the same amount of catalase and hydrogen peroxide, but each tube has a different pH. The student measures the oxygen produced in 2 minutes.
pH: 3, 5, 7, 9
Oxygen produced (mL): 4, 11, 28, 16
Which conclusion is best supported by the data?

choices.A: Catalase was most active at pH 7.
choices.B: Catalase was most active at pH 3.
choices.C: pH had no effect on catalase activity.
choices.D: Catalase produced the same oxygen amount at every pH.

questions.correctAnswerText: Catalase was most active at pH 7.
questions.explanation: The most oxygen, 28 mL, was produced at pH 7, showing the highest catalase activity.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
Pressure can be calculated as force divided by area. A student pushes four objects with the same force, but each object touches the floor over a different area.
Object | Force (N) | Contact area (m²)
A | 120 | 2
B | 120 | 3
C | 120 | 4
D | 120 | 6
Which object applies the greatest pressure to the floor?

choices.D: Object D

questions.correctAnswerText: Object A
questions.explanation: With the same force, the smallest contact area produces the greatest pressure. Object A has the smallest area.

questions.prompt:
Astronomers classify stars partly by color and temperature. Blue-white stars are generally hotter than yellow stars. Red stars are generally cooler than yellow stars. The Sun is a yellow star with a surface temperature of about 5,800 K.
Which star would most likely be hotter than the Sun?

choices.D: A rocky planet

questions.correctAnswerText: A blue-white star
questions.explanation: Blue-white stars are generally hotter than yellow stars such as the Sun.

questions.prompt:
The liver performs many functions that help maintain a stable internal environment. It processes nutrients absorbed from the digestive system, stores some glucose as glycogen, breaks down certain toxins, and produces bile that helps digest fats.
Which function of the liver is described as helping remove harmful substances from the body?

choices.D: Forming bones from calcium

questions.correctAnswerText: Breaking down certain toxins
questions.explanation: Breaking down toxins helps remove or reduce harmful substances in the body.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
A box applies a force of 600 N over an area of 3 m².
Pressure = force ÷ area
What pressure does the box apply in N/m²?
Type your answer as a whole number.
questions.explanation: Pressure = 600 N ÷ 3 m² = 200 N/m².

questions.prompt:
Select TWO answers.
A student investigates how the position of a fulcrum affects the force needed to lift a load with a lever. The student changes the fulcrum position but uses the same load and the same lever in each trial.
Which TWO factors should be kept constant?

choices.D: Force needed to lift the load

questions.correctAnswerText: Mass of the load | Length of the lever
questions.explanation: These factors should stay the same so fulcrum position is the main variable being tested.

questions.prompt: In some flowers, crossing a red-flowered plant with a white-flowered plant produces pink-flowered offspring. This pattern is called [D1] because the heterozygous offspring show [D2].
questions.correctAnswerText: D1=incomplete dominance; D2=an intermediate trait
questions.explanation: In incomplete dominance, heterozygous offspring show an intermediate phenotype, such as pink flowers.

questions.prompt:
A student tests a clear liquid with red and blue litmus paper. Blue litmus paper turns red, while red litmus paper stays red. The liquid also reacts with magnesium metal and produces bubbles of gas.
Which type of substance is most likely being tested?

choices.D: Inert gas

questions.correctAnswerText: Acid
questions.explanation: Acids turn blue litmus paper red and can react with some metals to produce gas.

questions.prompt:
A community compares energy sources. Petroleum forms from ancient organisms over millions of years and is used faster than it forms. Geothermal energy comes from heat inside Earth and can be used repeatedly in certain areas.
Which statement best identifies the nonrenewable resource?

choices.A: Petroleum is nonrenewable because it forms too slowly to be replaced on a human time scale.
choices.B: Geothermal energy is nonrenewable because Earth has no internal heat.
choices.C: Petroleum is renewable because it can be pumped from underground.
choices.D: Geothermal energy is renewable only if it is burned.

questions.correctAnswerText: Petroleum is nonrenewable because it forms too slowly to be replaced on a human time scale.
questions.explanation: Petroleum takes millions of years to form and is used much faster than it is naturally replaced.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
A wildlife biologist studies the population of deer in a fenced preserve. The preserve has limited food, water, and space.
Year | Deer population
1 | 45
2 | 81
3 | 126
4 | 149
5 | 153
6 | 151
Which conclusion is best supported by the data?

choices.A: The deer population appears to level off near the preserve’s carrying capacity.
choices.B: The deer population increases by the same number every year.
choices.C: The deer population reaches zero by Year 6.
choices.D: The preserve has unlimited resources for deer.

questions.correctAnswerText: The deer population appears to level off near the preserve’s carrying capacity.
questions.explanation: The population rises quickly at first and then levels near 150, suggesting a carrying capacity.

questions.prompt: Match each target with the correct tile.

tiles.A: Type of insulation wrapped around each cup
tiles.B: Temperature change of the water after 20 minutes
tiles.C: Same starting water temperature in every cup
tiles.D: Brand name printed on the cup

questions.correctAnswerText: T1=Type of insulation wrapped around each cup; T2=Temperature change of the water after 20 minutes; T3=Same starting water temperature in every cup
questions.explanation: The insulation type is changed, the temperature change is measured, and the starting temperature is controlled.

questions.prompt:
A person bends an arm at the elbow to lift a bag. The biceps muscle contracts while the triceps muscle relaxes. To straighten the arm again, the triceps contracts while the biceps relaxes.
Which statement best describes how these muscles work?

choices.A: They work as an opposing pair to move the arm in different directions.
choices.B: They both contract at the same time to prevent movement.
choices.C: They digest food before movement can happen.
choices.D: They carry oxygen through blood vessels.

questions.correctAnswerText: They work as an opposing pair to move the arm in different directions.
questions.explanation: The biceps and triceps contract and relax in opposite patterns to bend and straighten the arm.

questions.prompt:
A cold pack contains two substances in separate compartments. When the inner compartment is broken, the substances mix and the pack becomes cold. The reaction absorbs thermal energy from the surroundings.
Which statement best describes this reaction?

choices.A: It is endothermic because it absorbs thermal energy.
choices.B: It is exothermic because it absorbs thermal energy.
choices.D: It stops all particle motion immediately.

questions.correctAnswerText: It is endothermic because it absorbs thermal energy.
questions.explanation: A reaction that absorbs thermal energy from the surroundings is endothermic.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
Atmospheric CO₂ in one location increased from 400 ppm to 420 ppm over a period of years.
What was the percent increase?
Type your answer as a percent rounded to the nearest whole number.
questions.explanation: The increase is 20 ppm. Percent increase = 20 ÷ 400 × 100 = 5%.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
A researcher records urine output for a healthy adult during two conditions.
Condition | Water intake (L/day) | Urine output (L/day)
Low water intake | 1.0 | 0.6
High water intake | 2.5 | 1.9
Which conclusion is best supported by the data?

choices.A: Urine output increased when water intake increased.
choices.B: Urine output stayed the same in both conditions.
choices.C: The kidneys stopped working during high water intake.
choices.D: Higher water intake caused urine output to decrease to zero.

questions.correctAnswerText: Urine output increased when water intake increased.
questions.explanation: The table shows urine output increasing from 0.6 L/day to 1.9 L/day when water intake increased.

questions.prompt:
A student compares how well different wall materials reduce sound. The student places each material between a speaker and a sound meter. The speaker volume, speaker distance, and sound-meter position stay the same. The student records the sound level after the sound passes through each material.
What is the dependent variable?

choices.A: Sound level recorded by the meter
choices.D: Distance from speaker to meter

questions.correctAnswerText: Sound level recorded by the meter
questions.explanation: The dependent variable is the measured result after changing the wall material.

questions.prompt: A snowshoe spreads a person’s weight over a larger area than a regular boot. For the same weight, increasing the contact area [D1] the pressure on the snow, so the person is [D2] likely to sink deeply.
questions.correctAnswerText: D1=decreases; D2=less
questions.explanation: Spreading the same weight over a larger area decreases pressure, making sinking less likely.

questions.prompt:
A researcher studies how exercise intensity affects lactic acid levels in muscle cells. Volunteers complete light, moderate, and intense cycling sessions for the same amount of time. The researcher measures blood lactate immediately after each session.
Which factor is the independent variable?

choices.D: Type of measurement device

questions.correctAnswerText: Exercise intensity
questions.explanation: Exercise intensity is the factor intentionally changed by the researcher.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
A student builds circuits using one resistor and changes the voltage. The current is recorded.
Voltage (V): 2, 4, 6, 8
Current (A): 0.5, 1.0, 1.5, 2.0
Which statement best describes the relationship in the data?

choices.A: Current increases in direct proportion to voltage.
choices.C: Current stays the same as voltage changes.
choices.D: Current becomes zero when voltage reaches 8 V.

questions.correctAnswerText: Current increases in direct proportion to voltage.
questions.explanation: Current increases by 0.5 A for each 2 V increase, showing a direct proportional relationship.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
Select TWO answers.
A soil scientist compares two soil samples.
Soil sample | Sand (%) | Clay (%) | Water infiltration after 10 min (mL)
Sample A | 70 | 10 | 85
Sample B | 25 | 45 | 32
Which TWO statements are best supported by the data?

choices.A: Sample A allowed more water to infiltrate than Sample B.
choices.B: Sample B had a higher clay percentage than Sample A.
choices.C: Sample B allowed more water to infiltrate than Sample A.
choices.D: Sample A and Sample B had the same clay percentage.
choices.E: Sample A had less sand than Sample B.

questions.correctAnswerText: Sample A allowed more water to infiltrate than Sample B. | Sample B had a higher clay percentage than Sample A.
questions.explanation: Sample A had 85 mL of infiltration compared with 32 mL for Sample B, and Sample B had more clay.

questions.prompt:
Human blood type can be affected by multiple alleles. A person with type AB blood has one A allele and one B allele. Both alleles are expressed in the blood type instead of one completely hiding the other.
Which inheritance pattern is described?

choices.D: Evaporation

questions.correctAnswerText: Codominance
questions.explanation: In codominance, both alleles are expressed, as in type AB blood.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
A student uses a lever to lift a load. Mechanical advantage can be calculated as output force divided by input force.
Trial | Output force (N) | Input force (N)
A | 120 | 60
B | 120 | 40
C | 120 | 30
D | 120 | 20
Which trial has the greatest mechanical advantage?

choices.D: Trial D

questions.correctAnswerText: Trial D
questions.explanation: Mechanical advantage = output force ÷ input force. Trial D is 120 ÷ 20 = 6, the greatest value.

questions.prompt:
A text model describes the life cycle of a medium-sized star. A cloud of gas and dust collapses under gravity, forming a protostar. The star then spends most of its life fusing hydrogen in its core. Later, it expands into a red giant and eventually leaves behind a dense white dwarf.
According to the model, which stage occurs before the star becomes a main-sequence star?

choices.D: Black hole

questions.correctAnswerText: Protostar
questions.explanation: The model states that a protostar forms before the star spends most of its life fusing hydrogen.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
A clinic tests 250 blood samples. Of these samples, 35 have low iron levels.
What percent of the samples have low iron levels?
Type your answer as a percent rounded to the nearest whole number.

A mixture contains sand, salt, and iron filings. A student first uses a magnet to remove the iron filings. Then the student adds water, filters out the sand, and evaporates the water to recover the salt.
Which property allows the iron filings to be separated first?

choices.D: Color of sand

questions.correctAnswerText: Magnetism
questions.explanation: Iron filings are magnetic, so a magnet can separate them from the mixture.

questions.prompt:
A cell packages proteins into small sacs that can be moved to other parts of the cell or sent outside the cell. This packaging and shipping function is mainly performed by the Golgi apparatus.
Which statement best describes the Golgi apparatus?

choices.A: It modifies and packages proteins for transport.
choices.B: It stores most inherited information in the cell.
choices.D: It absorbs sunlight for photosynthesis in animal cells.

questions.correctAnswerText: It modifies and packages proteins for transport.
questions.explanation: The Golgi apparatus modifies, sorts, and packages proteins.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
A student pulls the same block across four surfaces and records the force needed to keep the block moving at a constant speed.
Surface | Pulling force (N)
Waxed wood | 6
Cardboard | 11
Rubber mat | 18
Rough carpet | 25
Which surface produced the greatest friction force?

choices.D: Rough carpet

questions.correctAnswerText: Rough carpet
questions.explanation: The largest pulling force, 25 N, was needed on rough carpet, indicating the greatest friction.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
A student compares two ground surfaces on a sunny day.
Surface | Percent sunlight reflected | Afternoon surface temperature (°C)
Light concrete | 42 | 31
Dark asphalt | 8 | 47
Which conclusion is best supported by the data?

choices.A: The darker surface reflected less sunlight and became warmer.
choices.B: The lighter surface reflected less sunlight and became warmer.
choices.C: Both surfaces reflected the same amount of sunlight.
choices.D: Reflection percentage had no relationship to surface temperature.

questions.correctAnswerText: The darker surface reflected less sunlight and became warmer.
questions.explanation: Dark asphalt reflected only 8% of sunlight and reached the higher temperature, 47°C.

questions.prompt:
Some fungi grow around plant roots. The fungi help the plant absorb minerals from the soil, and the plant provides the fungi with sugars made during photosynthesis. Both organisms benefit from the relationship.
Which type of relationship is described?

choices.D: Competition

questions.correctAnswerText: Mutualism
questions.explanation: Mutualism is a relationship in which both organisms benefit.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
A student tests three household liquids with an indicator that changes color at different pH levels.
Liquid | Indicator color | Approximate pH
Lemon juice | Red | 2
Milk | Yellow-green | 6
Soap solution | Blue | 10
Which liquid is most basic?

choices.D: All three liquids have the same pH

questions.correctAnswerText: Soap solution
questions.explanation: The soap solution has the highest pH, 10, making it the most basic.

questions.prompt:
Select TWO answers.
A student studies how soil moisture affects the hatching rate of reptile eggs. The student places equal numbers of eggs in containers with low, medium, and high soil moisture. Temperature and light exposure are kept the same.
Which TWO choices would improve the investigation?

choices.A: Use the same temperature for all containers.
choices.B: Repeat the test with several containers at each moisture level.
choices.C: Change both soil moisture and temperature at the same time.
choices.D: Count only the eggs that hatch first.
choices.E: Use different egg numbers in each container.

questions.correctAnswerText: Use the same temperature for all containers. | Repeat the test with several containers at each moisture level.
questions.explanation: Controlling temperature and repeating trials improves the reliability of the investigation.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
A device uses 48 joules of energy in 12 seconds.
Power = energy ÷ time
What is the power of the device in watts?
Type your answer as a whole number.
questions.explanation: Power = 48 J ÷ 12 s = 4 watts.

questions.prompt:
After a wildfire, grasses begin growing in an open area. Over several years, shrubs appear, followed by young trees. After many more years, the area may develop into a mature forest community if conditions allow.
Which ecological process is described?

choices.D: Blood clotting

questions.correctAnswerText: Succession
questions.explanation: Succession is the gradual change in a community over time after a disturbance or in a new area.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
A pedigree study tracks a trait in one family. The trait appears in every generation shown. An affected parent and an unaffected parent have four children: two affected and two unaffected. Both males and females can show the trait.
Which inheritance pattern is most consistent with this information?

choices.A: A dominant trait passed from affected parents to some offspring
choices.B: A trait caused only by lack of sunlight
choices.C: A trait that cannot be inherited
choices.D: A recessive trait that must skip every generation

questions.correctAnswerText: A dominant trait passed from affected parents to some offspring
questions.explanation: A trait that appears in every generation and is passed from an affected parent to about half the children is consistent with dominant inheritance.
